"I am in possession of complete works of Vivekananda" - well not surprising. 

Regarding Mahabharata - haven't read the text as such but can claim to be quite familiar with it, have seen the serial by B R Chopra thoroughly which in my view was very well made. 
Also, I read two books on it - one is Yuganta by Irawati Karve which gives a very different perspective to Mah.... (e.g. Yudhisthir was son of Vidur). Can mail u a pdf if you require.

Other one is  - the difficulty of being good by Gurcharan Das ex ceo of Procter & Gamble which tries to answer the question raised by Drapadi to Yudhishtir - why be good when being good comes with so many difficulties.  Available with me in hard form

I am any day game for this invigorating discourses we have.

I am in possession of complete works of Vivekananda as i consider his philosophy my GUIDING LIGHT in life since I was 19.
I am in possession of complete works of Vivekananda as i consider his philosophy my GUIDING LIGHT in life since I was 19.

I would request you to read the whole mahabharata , not just Gita. Gita was again popularised by British as they did not able to grasp the whole thing.

The appreciation for the language of Shakespeare could be relative. Somebody may not like the language at all. but the point about Shakespeare is his astute sense of politics and society which he represented through drama. For me that is the charm. Let us agree to differ but certainly keep alive our friendly yet highly scintillating chat !!!

Somebody may not like the language at all. but the point about Shakespeare is his astute sense of politics and society which he represented through drama. For me that is the charm. agree. there is no argument on this.
agree. there is no argument on this.
simple fables are represented in a simple way. they explore deep truths of life in a simpler way.
simple fables are represented in a simple way. they explore deep truths of life in a simpler way.

We continue misunderstanding each other, let me explain how.

There are two aspects 
- one is the language/the way it is said 
- the other is the message/the underlying meaning/the philosophy 

its the former in which I rate Shakespeare so highly. Recollect my comment on way he has expressed overconfidence - yr wisdom is consumed in confidence - it is this aspect of Shakespeare which I appreciate. 

In summary its not the philosophy where I rate him highly but just the way he writes, the expressions he uses. On the philosophical aspect I am very more attuned to the Indian/Eastern/Oriental philosophy and would like/love to spread the msg that Vivekananda gave to the world in his famous Addresses at The Parliament of Religions (link http://tinyurl.com/395rwlw).

Also I have been looking for someone who can help me understand the Gita more from a knowledge and action angle I have tried reading it a few times in hindi in enlgish, gone thru some of the commentaries but not satisfied with my effort. 

Regards
Pawan Lohia

those who cannot read or choose not to read Shakespeare are not at fault. do in USA majority people read Tagore or even Shakespeare ? They do not. Reading Shakespeare is deep and reading Panchatantra is not deep tantamount to elitism. I practice neither elitism nor populism. I deplore both.

those who cannot read or choose not to read Shakespeare are not at fault. do in USA majority people read Tagore or even Shakespeare ? They do not. Reading Shakespeare is deep and reading Panchatantra is not deep tantamount to elitism. I practice neither elitism nor populism. I deplore both.

No you completely misunderstood me. I enjoy this tet a tet. For me Shakespeare is not that highly placed compared to fables or panchatantra. i get pleasure and  succour from both. It depends! But you seem to place epistemologically  Shakespeare on a higher pedestal. This is to me orientalism and a classic post colonial predicament.

No you completely misunderstood me. I enjoy this tet a tet. For me Shakespeare is not that highly placed compared to fables or panchatantra. i get pleasure and  succour from both. It depends! But you seem to place epistemologically  Shakespeare on a higher pedestal. This is to me orientalism and a classic post colonial predicament.

I think I have irritated you. 

The issue is when something comes from someone who quotes Shakespeare and writes things which I, at times, have to read and reread to decipher, the expectations automatically become much higher (or shud I say much deeper meaning wise) and even in simple fables one starts looking for greater and deeper meanings because of this.

In future, I shall be more discerning.

Regards
Pawan Lohia

I have Sri Sri Academy which meets all the criteria set by you except that its far from my home (Salkia Howrah). But getting admission in upper nursery over here too is becoming a big challenge The only option in Howrah is St. Agnes but its a girl's school. I have a preference for ICSE over CBSE, am I right?

Also, if you could suggest some schools it will be really helpful. 

Regards
Pawan Lohia

Dear Mr. Lohia:
                          My sentiments exactly. Enjoying our interactions very much. Feeling sad to hear about your young boss.

                         I think you may  consider of the following points before choosing a school for your daughter. the school must have a playground ; strong on extra-curricular activities; must be a co-ed school; must not be too far from where you live; and of course, there is no reputation of any teacher in the school as child -harasser, i.e. there is no teacher in the school with a reputation that s/he is too strict. The last one is a tall order but you can try. The rest is beyond anybody's control. 

                         As parent though you can inculcate values in your child which will make her confident, strong yet polite and soft. And finally, do not impose neither yours nor anybody's unfulfilled dreams on your child. allow space to her to grow.

                         Hoping the best for you all. Thank you for being a conscious and conscientious parent.

best,

bula bhadra

The last one is a tall order but you can try. The rest is beyond anybody's control.

                         Hoping the best for you all. Thank you for being a conscious and conscientious parent.

Respected Mam,

Its wonderful to interact with you thru this medium.  I had a boss Mr. Rahul Roy youngest person to become the President of the CA institute. I used to have similar interactions with him. There were times when real life interactions  where based on Julius Caesar. He expired last year at an age of just 47 These interactions with you reminiscent of him for there are very few around with whom you can have rich literary discussions without any objective/agenda in mind. 

I had interacted with you at the NDTV discussion on the La Marts issue at Townhall and was quite impressed with your revolutionary views on education. I am currently trying for my daughter's admissions (she is 3+). In case it is not inconvenient for you, I want to seek your guidance/advise/counselling on schools which would be most suitable for her. pls let me know

Apologies for a lengthy message

Regards
Pawan Lohia
9903800354
